posts.text:
The @IEA reckons that UAE reached ~85% of pre-war oil exports in early June (ie, before the MoU was signed) thanks to a combination of bypass pipeline, Fujairah strategic storage and, importantly, dark SoH crossing along the Omani coastline. 

In early June, IEA puts UAE total https://t.co/YknPiqGZ3J
posts.summary: The UAE recovered approximately 85% of its pre-war oil export capacity by early June through alternative routes including bypass pipelines, strategic storage facilities, and ship-to-ship transfers along the Omani coast, demonstrating resilience in maintaining crude supply despite regional disruptions.

posts.text:
Oil Tanker Booked in Gulf at 897% of Benchmark Freight Rate

 A supertanker has been provisionally booked to transport oil from the Persian Gulf to India at a rate equivalent to almost nine times benchmark freight costs, an eye-watering price that reflects the shortage of
posts.summary: A supertanker has been booked to transport oil from the Persian Gulf to India at nearly nine times the benchmark freight rate, indicating a severe shortage of available tanker capacity. The exceptionally high freight costs reflect tight supply conditions in the oil shipping market.

posts.text: $XOM Exxon Set to Halt Its Biggest European Refinery Due to Strike https://t.co/F9mFqpqhjg
posts.summary: Exxon is expected to shut down its largest European refinery due to a strike, which could reduce refined product supply in European markets and potentially support crude oil prices. The disruption may create supply tightness for gasoline, diesel, and other refined products in the region.

posts.text: The volume of oil sitting on ships in the Persian Gulf has fallen from ~160 million barrels at the beginning of June to ~110 million barrels today.
posts.summary: Oil inventory stored on vessels in the Persian Gulf has declined by approximately 50 million barrels since early June, suggesting either increased demand, reduced supply, or both. This drawdown in floating storage could indicate tightening market conditions or a shift in crude oil trading patterns.

posts.text:
Since the signing of the US-Iran MOU, oil flows through Hormuz have regularly risen back to 10-15 MMbpd.

Factoring for Hormuz crisis reroutes, that's effectively pre-war levels.

BUT fresh Gulf loadings are still sub-5 MMbpd, way too low to sustain these flows for long. https://t.co/XuLcotxo6N
posts.summary: Oil shipments through the Strait of Hormuz have recovered to pre-war levels following the US-Iran agreement, but insufficient new crude loading from Gulf producers suggests this flow rate may not be sustainable long-term.

posts.text:
Iran has exported 40 million barrels of crude oil since 2026-06-15. Exactly half of that amount shipped out in a single day; which was last Friday (2026-06-19).

#OOTT #IranWar #Tankers
posts.summary: Iran exported 40 million barrels of crude oil between mid-June and late June 2026, with half of that volume shipped in a single day on June 19. This unusual concentration of exports in one day suggests a significant spike in Iranian oil market activity during that period.

posts.text:
Hormuz traffic cautiously improves

Confirmed Strait of Hormuz traffic stayed active on 23 June, with 31 verified crossings across commercial and energy-linked vessels. West-to-east movements dominated, while Iranian, Omani and IMO routes all remained in use. The Strait appears https://t.co/TOe354A89q
posts.summary: The Strait of Hormuz maintained steady vessel traffic with 31 crossings on June 23, suggesting continued stability in a critical chokepoint for global oil shipments. Sustained activity across multiple shipping routes indicates no significant disruption to energy flows through the strait.

posts.text: Trump is now turning his attention to refiners and fuel producers, criticizing the slow pace of declines in retail gasoline prices following the sharp drop in crude oil. However, the refining sector's response remains constrained by historically low inventories of refined https://t.co/qPgOM4srBS https://t.co/lyha5bPb9A
posts.summary: Trump is criticizing fuel producers for not passing crude oil price declines through to retail gasoline prices quickly enough, though the refining sector's ability to respond is limited by historically low refined product inventories.

posts.text:
WTI front-month contracts are on the cusp of falling into the $60's as the market is pricing in the return of ⁠Iranian oil re-entering the global market and a normalization of Strait of Hormuz volumes.

Given the significant drain in inventories, this could be a bit too https://t.co/gmcaHwC1gz
posts.summary: WTI crude oil is approaching the $60 per barrel level as markets anticipate Iranian oil supplies returning to global markets and normalized Strait of Hormuz shipping volumes. However, the significant drawdown in inventories may make such a price decline excessive given current supply tightness.

posts.text:
EIA inventory report:

U.S. commercial crude inventories fell by 6 million barrels, while the Strategic Petroleum Reserve declined by a further 9 million barrels to a fresh multi-decade low. Combined, total U.S. crude stockpiles have fallen by around 100 million barrels since https://t.co/YYb4R7ie68
posts.summary: U.S. crude inventories are declining significantly, with commercial stocks down 6 million barrels and the Strategic Petroleum Reserve hitting multi-decade lows, indicating tightening oil supply conditions. The combined 100 million barrel reduction in total U.S. crude stockpiles could support higher oil prices in the near term.

posts.text: refineries in Europe melting faster than a snowcone in Phoenix- when temps get THIS hot- efficiency drops- need more cooling capacity as units will run too hot- output then declines. same would happen in 100F weather in the northern US- refineries not equipped for intense heat
posts.summary: European refineries are experiencing efficiency losses due to extreme heat, requiring additional cooling capacity to maintain output levels, with similar constraints potentially affecting northern US refineries during intense heat events. This could result in reduced refinery output and tighter oil product supplies during high-temperature periods.

posts.text: if gas prices had no lag to oil, the national average today could be in the ballpark of $3.50-$3.70/gal, but reality is we're currently at $3.90/gal as prices are sticky on the way down as stations take their time due to margin lost in Mar/Apr when prices spiked
posts.summary: Gas prices are currently lagging behind oil price declines due to stations maintaining higher margins to recover losses from the March-April price spike, resulting in a national average around $3.90 per gallon instead of the $3.50-$3.70 range that immediate oil price correlation would suggest.

posts.summary: Strait of Hormuz traffic showed signs of recovery on June 23 with 31 verified vessel crossings, suggesting improved operational conditions for crude oil transit through this critical shipping chokepoint.

posts.text:
Oil investment of $17.7 trillion required to meet demand in the long term.

Total cumulative oil investments to meet demand requirements add up to $17.7 trillion
(US$2026) for the period 2026–2050. For the upstream, investment requirements are assessed at $14.5 trillion. https://t.co/lvRY7jPW3r
posts.summary: OPEC estimates that the global oil industry will need $17.7 trillion in cumulative investments between 2026 and 2050 to meet long-term demand, with upstream operations accounting for $14.5 trillion of that total. The figure underscores the substantial capital requirements necessary to sustain oil supply levels over the coming decades.

posts.text:
Global energy spending is on track to reach $3.4 trillion in 2026

Around $2.2 trillion of this is set to go to grids, storage, low-emissions fuels, nuclear, renewables, efficiency & electrification

And about $1.2 trillion goes to oil, natural gas & coal: https://t.co/8IajZj1gIL https://t.co/9SrWhBJOx8
posts.summary: Global energy spending through 2026 will direct approximately $1.2 trillion toward oil, natural gas, and coal, representing about one-third of total energy investment as the majority shifts toward clean energy infrastructure. This allocation reflects ongoing but declining investment in fossil fuels relative to low-emissions alternatives.

posts.text: Share prices for two of America's largest gasoline retailers, $MUSA and $CASY, are down 5-6 percent this morning after President Trump threatened to use the Department of Justice to investigate gasoline prices.

posts.text: EIA: Miserable report for refiners with 3.1 m build in distillate stocks and 2.1-m build in gasoline. Gas demand measurement of 8.775-mbd will restart demand destruction dialogue. But refiners, blenders and importers are no longer bringing 10-mbd of gas to market, so be careful.
posts.summary: The EIA report showed significant builds in distillate and gasoline inventories alongside declining gasoline demand, pressuring refiners while supply constraints limit market flexibility. With gasoline supply below 10 million barrels per day, the combination of weak demand and constrained refining capacity could create market tightness despite current inventory builds.

posts.text:
